page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22-lssd is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 22 vCPUs mapped to 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 88 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per vCPU. Storage is bundled with four local NVMe SSDs totaling 1608 GB of high-speed local storage capacity.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1622.88 and a multi-core score of 18316.01. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 1499.45 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 109.64 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in workloads like cryptography and low-latency inference. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 293.95 tokens per second with a 135M parameter model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the inclusion of 1.6 TB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a balanced CPU-to-memory ratio offers high resource density for data-intensive applications without requiring external block storage. This hardware profile is well-suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing. The lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training, but the integrated AMX instructions provide a viable alternative for smaller CPU-based inference tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22-lssd is a general-purpose server configured with 22 vCPUs and 88 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. It features 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives, offering high-speed local data access. The server delivers balanced compute and memory performance, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 12136.0 and a static web serving throughput of 31.08 GB/sec. It supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which enhance cryptographic and matrix math operations. This instance is optimized for database workloads, web serving, and software compilation where local high-speed storage is critical.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22-lssd is a general-purpose server featuring 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-22-lssd features 22 vCPUs, 88 GB RAM, and 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 22 vCPUs and 88 GB RAM
Includes 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated matrix and vector math
Delivers 31.08 GB/sec static web serving throughput in benchmarks
Best suited for database operations, web serving, and software compilation
